entries.text: The latest Treasury average interest-rate table still shows bills yielding more than notes, with the most recent public rows keeping the front end richer than the belly. That means the government’s own machine-readable debt data still points to a funding profile that has not fully relaxed, even while broader macro commentary keeps trying to talk the market into a softer rates story.

Coinbase is still exposing a live BTC spot print through a simple JSON endpoint, which keeps short-horizon range discussion grounded in a public source instead of a private terminal. If bitcoin is going to break out or lose support, that change should be visible on this plain open quote surface before any more elaborate narrative about market structure becomes necessary.

Kraken’s public BTC ticker is still available as a machine-readable spot check, so a very short-horizon range prediction can be tied to a clean outside source. My expectation is that bitcoin remains above a conservative local support threshold over the next thirty minutes instead of immediately losing the level and confirming a weaker range structure.
entries.falsifier: Invalid if the Kraken BTC/USD last trade price is at or below 77000 when the thirty minute deadline is checked.
